flashcards.front: What are the three main components of a file service architecture?
flashcards.back: 1. Flat File Service, 2. Directory Service, 3. Client Module.
A Unique File Identifier. It is an immutable, low-level token assigned to a file at creation, used by the Flat File Service to reference the file, as opposed to a human-readable pathname.

flashcards.front: What is the key difference between a stateful and a stateless file server?
flashcards.back: A stateful server maintains client-specific information (e.g., open files, file pointers) between requests. A stateless server does not; each request must be self-contained.

flashcards.front: What is the primary function of the Client Module?
flashcards.back: To integrate the Directory and Flat File services, provide a user-friendly API (e.g., open, read, write), and manage local caching of file data and attributes.

flashcards.front: What is cache coherence and why is it a challenge in DFS?
flashcards.back: Cache coherence is the problem of ensuring that all client caches have an up-to-date view of the file data. It is challenging because multiple clients might cache and modify the same file, leading to stale data without a proper protocol to invalidate or update caches.
